questions.q: In which year was the States Reorganisation Act enacted?
questions.explanation: The States Reorganisation Act was enacted in 1956.

questions.q: The States Reorganisation Act reorganised the boundaries of India's states largely along which basis?
questions.explanation: The Act reorganised state boundaries largely along linguistic lines.

questions.q: The States Reorganisation Act was based on the recommendations of which commission?
questions.explanation: The Act was based on the recommendations of the States Reorganisation Commission.

questions.q: The main effect of the States Reorganisation Act was to:
options: Redraw the internal map of India's states
questions.explanation: The Act redrew the boundaries of India's states, redrawing the country's internal map.

questions.q: The States Reorganisation Act dealt primarily with which level of India's federal structure?
questions.explanation: The Act reorganised the states of the Indian Union.

questions.q: Reorganising states 'along linguistic lines' means grouping areas mainly by which shared feature?
questions.explanation: Linguistic reorganisation grouped regions largely according to the language commonly spoken there.

questions.q: The States Reorganisation Act was passed during which decade?
questions.explanation: The Act was passed in 1956, during the 1950s.

questions.q: A 'commission' such as the one behind the Act is best described as a body that:
options: Studies an issue and makes recommendations
questions.explanation: The States Reorganisation Commission studied state boundaries and recommended changes that the Act implemented.

questions.q: The States Reorganisation Act is an example of action taken by which lawmaking body?
questions.explanation: As an Act, it was enacted by the Parliament of India.

questions.q: The subtitle 'Redrawing the Map' for the States Reorganisation Act refers to changing the map of:
questions.explanation: The Act redrew the internal map of India by altering state boundaries.

questions.q: The States Reorganisation Act of 1956 was passed how many years after India became a republic?
questions.explanation: India became a republic in 1950, and the Act came six years later in 1956.

questions.q: Before the 1956 reorganisation, India's units were classified into categories such as Part A, B and C states. The Act largely replaced these with:
options: A simpler scheme of states and union territories
questions.explanation: The Act replaced the earlier Part A, B and C classification with a clearer arrangement of states and union territories.

questions.q: The States Reorganisation Commission whose recommendations led to the Act was set up in which year?
questions.explanation: The States Reorganisation Commission was constituted in 1953, reporting before the 1956 Act.

questions.q: Which southern state was created in 1956 as part of, and around the time of, the linguistic reorganisation, uniting Kannada-speaking areas?
questions.explanation: The reorganisation united Kannada-speaking areas into the enlarged Mysore State, later renamed Karnataka.

questions.q: Which Malayalam-speaking state was formed in 1956 as part of the reorganisation?
questions.explanation: Kerala was formed in 1956 by bringing together Malayalam-speaking regions.

questions.q: The demand for linguistic states gained momentum after the creation, in 1953, of which state for Telugu speakers?
questions.explanation: Andhra State was created in 1953 for Telugu speakers, spurring wider demands that the 1956 Act addressed.

questions.q: The States Reorganisation Act primarily altered which kind of boundaries, rather than India's external borders?
questions.explanation: The Act changed the internal boundaries dividing India's states, not the nation's external borders.

questions.q: Implementing the States Reorganisation Act also required which kind of legal instrument to alter the Constitution's list of states?
questions.explanation: A constitutional amendment accompanied the Act to revise the schedule listing India's states and territories.

questions.q: The reorganisation under the 1956 Act reflected which guiding idea about how states might be organised?
options: That states should have equal area
That states should match river basins exactly
That a shared language could form a basis for a state
That states should be drawn by lottery
questions.explanation: The reorganisation embodied the idea that a common language could serve as a principle for forming states.

questions.q: The 1956 reorganisation took effect under the framework of the Constitution, which describes India as a:
questions.explanation: The Constitution describes India as a 'Union of States', within which the 1956 reorganisation occurred.

questions.q: The States Reorganisation Act is categorised in this deck as which type of entry?
questions.explanation: The Act is presented as a landmark event in India's political history.

questions.q: The States Reorganisation Commission of 1953 was chaired by which person?
questions.explanation: The States Reorganisation Commission was chaired by Fazl Ali, with K.M. Panikkar and H.N. Kunzru as members.

questions.q: An earlier committee, the JVP Committee that examined linguistic states before 1953, took its name from the initials of which three leaders?
options: Jagjivan Ram, V.V. Giri and Pratibha Patil
Jayaprakash Narayan, V.P. Singh and P.V. Narasimha Rao
Jawaharlal Nehru, Vallabhbhai Patel and Pattabhi Sitaramayya
Jagjivan Ram, Vinoba Bhave and Pant
questions.explanation: The JVP Committee was named for Jawaharlal Nehru, Vallabhbhai Patel and Pattabhi Sitaramayya.

questions.q: The bilingual state of Bombay, which the 1956 Act retained, was later split in 1960 into which two states?
questions.explanation: Bombay State was bifurcated in 1960 into Maharashtra and Gujarat, continuing the linguistic reorganisation.

questions.q: The constitutional amendment that accompanied the 1956 Act to revise the list of states was the:
questions.explanation: The Seventh Constitutional Amendment of 1956 accompanied the States Reorganisation Act to update the state list.

questions.q: Under the 1956 reorganisation, the older fourfold classification of states was reduced, and the new union territories were administered chiefly by which authority?
questions.explanation: Union territories created under the scheme were administered by the Union government rather than having their own full statehood.

questions.q: The States Reorganisation Act came into effect in the same year, 1956, as which other significant reform of state administration?
options: The introduction of Electronic Voting Machines
The abolition of the Rajya Sabha
The Seventh Amendment reclassifying states and union territories
questions.explanation: Both the States Reorganisation Act and the Seventh Amendment, restructuring states and union territories, took effect in 1956.

questions.q: The constitutional provision empowering Parliament to form new states and alter boundaries, used in such reorganisations, is found around which article?
questions.explanation: Article 3 empowers Parliament to form new states and alter the boundaries or names of existing states.

questions.q: The 1956 reorganisation is regarded as a major milestone because it addressed long-standing demands without altering which core feature of the Republic?
questions.explanation: The reorganisation rearranged internal units while keeping intact the single, unified framework of the Indian Union.

questions.q: The process the 1956 Act set in motion, of forming states on a linguistic basis, was later continued by the creation of which state in 1966 for Punjabi speakers?
questions.explanation: In 1966 the former Punjab was reorganised on linguistic lines into Punjab and Haryana, continuing the 1956 process.
